name: camellia sinensis
mechanism: Catechins (especially EGCG) and methylxanthines modulate adenosine signaling, catechol metabolism, endothelial function, and oxidative stress pathways.
effects: No direct effects data. Contextual inference:
nan.. No direct mechanismofaction data. Contextual inference:
contraindications: Use caution with prescription medications and chronic conditions; monitor for intolerance.
safetyNotes: Moderate intake is generally safe; concentrated extracts have been linked to rare hepatotoxicity. Excess caffeine may worsen insomnia, anxiety, palpitations, or reflux.

sources.title: Green tea extract-associated hepatotoxicity: systematic review (Arch Toxicol. 2018)
sources.note: Best available synthesis of hepatic safety signals.

sources.title: Green Tea - NCCIH
sources.note: Safety and interaction overview including concentrated extracts.

interactions: Caffeine-containing products can add sympathomimetic effects with other stimulants.
May reduce warfarin effect when vitamin K intake from green tea is high and variable.
Can reduce absorption of non-heme iron and may affect some beta-blocker exposures.
